title: Thames water crisis deepens after government objects to £10bn rescue plan
summary_full: Britain's largest water supplier, Thames Water, faces potential government control as ministers question a £10 billion rescue plan.  Serving 16 million, the debt-laden company could enter a temporary nationalisation if regulators reject the creditor-led proposal.  Concerns centre on customer burdens and environmental protections amid years of mismanagement.
